title: limnophila gratioloides
intro: Limnophila gratioloides, also known as Vietnamese coriander or sweet basil, is a plant species belonging to the family Plantaginaceae. Traditionally used in Southeast Asian cuisine, it is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Limnophila gratioloides include its ability to support digestive health, alleviate symptoms of respiratory issues, and exhibit potential antiseptic properties. Limnophila gratioloides can be consumed in various herbal preparations, including teas, infusions, and extracts, making it a valuable addition to traditional medicine.
